title: Thermal management for inductive charging pad
abstract: An inductive charging pad with active thermal management using a temperature sensor and a cooling fan controlled to keep the coil below a temperature limit.
claims: An inductive charging pad comprising: a primary coil; a temperature sensor thermally coupled to the primary coil; a cooling fan; and a controller configured to drive the cooling fan and to limit power to the primary coil when a measured temperature exceeds a threshold.
The pad of claim 1, wherein the temperature sensor is a thermistor.
The pad of claim 1, wherein the controller reduces a duty cycle of the primary coil when the threshold is exceeded.
source: DEMO FIXTURE (synthetic, not a real patent)
notes: Synthetic demo sample for the triage UI; not a real USPTO record.
